figures.caption: Fig. 1: Multiscale super-resolution demo using 200+ µm pixels to reconstruct images comparable to 13 µm pixel.
Fig. 12: Experimental demonstration of how using multiple imaging scales improves super-resolution reconstructions.
Fig. 11: Three experimental demonstrations of mutliscale super-resolution using CCD binning at three image scales.

Fig. 8: Experimental measurements of 1D linearly increasing grating for bin sizes 4, 8, 16, 20, and 24. Each binned measurement (orange line) is plotted alongside the single-pixel measurement (blue line) so that the diminishing contrast and aliasing can be directly observed.
Fig. 10: Comparison of (a) predicted reconstruction error under i.i.d. Noise with (b) experimentally observed error when using coprime box sizes, k1 and k2.
